figures.caption: TABLE II ENVIRONMENT AND EPISODE PARAMETERS. VALUES FOLLOW HIGHWAY-ENV DEFAULTS FOR HIGHWAY-FAST-V0 EXCEPT WHERE LABELED OURS. SEEDS ARE A FIXED SAMPLE DRAWN FROM THE DEFAULT SEED BANK WITHOUT SCENARIO STRATIFICATION; THE MATCHED-SEED DESIGN IS REPORTED AT THE CANONICAL n=10, 20-STEP SCALE. NOTE: REPORTED RUNS APPLY THE REALISTIC-HIGHWAY SPEED PROFILE (TARGET BAND 0–130 KM/H, REWARD BAND 80–130 KM/H); THE DISCRETE {0, 5, . . . , 30} M/S TARGETS ABOVE ARE THE SIMULATOR’S PRIMITIVE ACTION PROXIES.

TABLE III MAIN N10/20-STEP SCREENING RESULTS. RED ROWS ARE NEGATIVE EVIDENCE FOR DEPLOYABILITY; THE CLEAN CLAUDE HAIKU 4.5 DUAL-AGENT ROW IS PENDING AND OMITTED. BOLD MARKS THE BEST COMPARABLE DEPLOYABLE VALUE AND UNDERLINING MARKS THE SECOND-BEST VALUE WITHIN A BLOCK.

Fig. 1. Overall Steins;Gate Drive architecture. A compact highway state and feasible primitive actions define role-typed world-line branches. Analytical scoring produces a short selector-visible set; the strategic LLM commits one branch as a typed StrategicForecast. The canonical dual-agent runtime stores this object in a forecast buffer, tracks WorldlineExecutionState, and emits a constrained TacticalDecision; validity, abort, drift, or safety pressure triggers override or strategic refresh.

TABLE IV MECHANISM SENSITIVITY SWEEPS FOR STEINS;GATE DRIVE USING GPT-5.4 MINI ON MATCHED NORMAL HIGHWAY-FAST-V0 N10/20-STEP RUNS. EACH BLOCK VARIES ONE MECHANISM KNOB AND REPORTS SAFETY, SPEED, EFFECTIVE LAG, AND LOWER-SCORE SELECTION.

Fig. 2. World-line generation roles in a highway-env-style highway scene. Alpha branches roll out each feasible primitive action with ego kinematics and local gaps. Beta branches reuse the same action-conditioned future but stress a selected neighboring actor. Gamma branches reuse the action-conditioned future but apply a configured hazard. The generator produces a bounded, typed branch set in which every future has a reproducible source and paper-facing role tag.

TABLE V ROLE-SET MECHANISM AUDIT FOR THE DUAL-AGENT STEINS;GATE DRIVE SELECTOR. VALUES ARE FROM THE GPT-5.4 MINI REALISTIC-HIGHWAY N10/20-STEP SWEEP. THE FIRST FOUR COLUMNS ARE BALANCED PRESENTATIONS THAT EXPOSE AT LEAST ONE BRANCH OF EACH ENABLED ROLE TO THE SELECTOR; THE FIFTH COLUMN (NAT.) KEEPS THE IMPLEMENTATION’S UNBALANCED ROLE AVAILABILITY. BOLD MARKS THE BEST COMPARABLE VALUE AND UNDERLINING THE SECOND-BEST FOR DIRECTED METRIC ROWS.
